Feedback: Age range: sedaDNA often use age depth models, note that this should be described in descriptions/methods, and refer to use the C14 checklist when necessary

For the “sample age range oldest/youngest unit”, it is currently possible to select several options (as written under guidance in the column help). However, for “sample age range oldest/youngest limit”, the possibility of inputting several options is not referred to either in the description or the column guidance. This inconsistency should be addressed by updating the description and examples for “sample age range oldest/youngest limit” to reflect the possibility of multiple entries. It was also questioned whether allowing multiple entries is necessary, as values expressed in different units (e.g., BCE and BP) can be converted if one of them is provided.
Comments: ACtually decided better to flip it: multivalued dates and only single system, because better to have consistency while maintaining single-type term/columns.

Feedback: Is it possible to suggest being more precise, such as for instance ‘Upper Holocene’? The group was suggesting this so that search for a specific time period such as Upper Holocene would be easier to find.

(comment applicable to "local environmental context") Applying these terms to archaeological samples can be problematic, as many archaeological contexts (e.g. urban environments) are not adequately represented. Instead of being required, they should be recommended. A glossary can be used (as suggested in the definition) but terms that are adapted to archaeological contexts and environments should be added. Several participants spent a certain amount of time trying to determine what to enter for these fields.
